mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital expenditure channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through robust earnings growth.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below historical expansion thresholds reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term output declines.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of observations when INDPRO falls below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive discount rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPI and high rates yields positive forward returns in >65% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings alignment.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals immediate labor market contraction and reduced household income, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate weaker consumer-driven earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS crosses below prior month by > 50k
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the payroll decline signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stable retail sales indicates inventory buildup and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved operational efficiency and margin expansion.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict 5-day returns with a t-statistic > 1.96 in out-of-sample tests.
